title: Filing a tax return
description: Practice understanding of what is needed to file a tax return, from personal information to official and non-official tax documents.

assessment_items.question_content:
Filing tax returns begins with entering personal information.

**Which personal information do you need to file a tax return?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Filing tax returns begins with entering personal information.

**What do you need to file a tax return?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You are a parent preparing to file your family’s tax return.

**What personal information do you need to collect before you file?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: social security/tax payer identification numbers for everyone

assessment_items.question_content:
**Identify the type of document and complete the statement below.**

[[☃ image 1]]

This [[☃ dropdown 1]] a tax document and you [[☃ dropdown 2]] need it to file a tax return.
hints: To file a tax return, you will need personal information, as well as official and non-official tax documents and forms.
Official tax documents and forms typically have a name that is a combination of letters and numbers (like W-2).

The document shows 1098-T, which means it is an official tax form.

[[☃ image 1]]

This $\boxed{\text{is}}$
a tax document and you $\boxed{\text{will}}$
need it to file a tax return.

options.alt: a document that reads 2024, form 1098-T. It lists a fictional university name and payments in the amount of 8,443 dollars for qualified tuition.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Identify the type of document and complete the statement below.**

[[☃ image 1]]

This [[☃ dropdown 1]] a tax document and you [[☃ dropdown 2]] need it to file a tax return.
hints: To file a tax return, you will need personal information, as well as official and non-official tax documents and forms.
Official tax documents and forms typically have a name that is a combination of letters and numbers (like W-2).

The document shows 1099-NEC, which means it is an official tax form.

[[☃ image 1]]

This $\boxed{\text{is}}$
a tax document and you $\boxed{\text{will}}$
need it to file a tax return.

options.alt: a document that reads 2024, form 1099-NEC. It lists Khan Academy as the employer name and payments in the amount of 1,000 dollars for qualified compensation.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Identify the type of document and complete the statement below.**

[[☃ image 1]]

This [[☃ dropdown 1]] a tax document and you [[☃ dropdown 2]] need it to file a tax return.
hints: To file a tax return, you will need personal information, as well as official and non-official tax documents and forms.
Official tax documents and forms typically have a name that is a combination of letters and numbers (like W-2).

While it does say *tax* in several places, this document is **not** an official tax form. 

Tax prep fee is also not one of the expenses you can deduct, so the IRS does not ask for this document. 

[[☃ image 1]]

This $\boxed{\text{is not}}$
a tax document and you $\boxed{\text{will not}}$
need it to file a tax return.

options.alt: a document that reads Tax Prep. It lists Emma Litoff as a customer name with a customer number W08834267 and a payment in the amount of 75 dollars for tax preparation.

assessment_items.question_content:
Knowing which documents you need to file a tax return can save you a lot of time and frustration.

**Which of these do you need to file a tax return?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Knowing which documents you need to file a tax return can save you a lot of time and frustration.

**Which of these do you need to file a tax return?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Knowing which documents you need to file a tax return can save you a lot of time and frustration.

**Which of these do you need to file a tax return?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Organizing your tax documents makes it a lot easier for you or your tax preparer to file your taxes.

**What is a good way to organize your documents for tax filing?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Download all your tax forms and documents into a single folder.
Store all your mail in a single drawer.
Write down all your tax information on a sticky note.

assessment_items.question_content:
Organizing your tax documents makes it a lot easier for you or your tax preparer to file your taxes.

**What is a good way to organize your documents for tax filing?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Use a folder to store all relevant documents and receipts.
Keep all your mail in a single drawer.
Document all of your tax information on a sticky note.
Collect all of your receipts in a box.

assessment_items.question_content:
Organizing your tax documents makes it a lot easier for you or your tax preparer to file your taxes.

**What is a good way to be organized for tax filing?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Put all records, receipts, and relevant documents into a box.
Use a filing cabinet and drop all your mail in it.
Write down all of your tax information in a notebook.
Collect all your receipts for the year.
